stemHtml: What is true about elevated runway lights?

options.a: Elevated lights must be frangible, must provide clearance for engine pods and propellers, and stopway lights must be flush with the ground.
options.b: Elevated lights must be rigid to withstand engine blast and propeller wash.
options.c: Rigid lighting is required only on taxiways and stopways to prevent displacement.
options.d: All elevated lights, including those on the stopway, must be frangible and positioned to avoid obstruction to aircraft engines and propellers.
